lane_1_scout_refinement.selected_feedback: Fresh first-session menu focus still defaults to world-map even when the guided starter target is the current Sunny Beach opener.
evidence: .agents/project-memory.md says starter-stage menu focus should only default to world-map when guided nextBiomeId differs from the current biome.
src/engine/game.ts has the guarded starter check, then falls through to the generic in-biome world-map default.
src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts still expects world-map for the fresh nextBiomeId: beach starter state.
main_contract: Restore the intended fresh/current-biome starter default so it does not select world-map before Shore Shelter.
Keep the post-Shore Shelter next-habitat default on world-map and keep station-return/season-close world-map menus defaulting to field-station.
Refresh only focused runtime-smoke coverage for this first-session menu-focus behavior.
non_goals: No route, station, save, world-map focus-order, field-guide export, geometry, science content, or tooling changes.
No new tutorial panel, HUD, or onboarding state.

lane_1_main_result.summary: Fresh/current-biome starter menus now prefer field-guide instead of world-map, while post-Shore-Shelter next-habitat and station-return defaults remain covered.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "arms sound after input"
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "shows notebook prompts in the journal and reuses them in the copied field-guide prompt"
lane_1_main_result.known_noise: Full runtime-smoke remains red in the dirty tree on broader map-focus / route-replay expectations and an unrelated High Pass rime-footing copy expectation outside this item.

lane_1_critic_result.summary: No blocker found. The fresh/current-biome starter menu no longer selects world-map, while the later guided world-map and station-return defaults remain covered.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "arms sound after input"
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "shows notebook prompts in the journal and reuses them in the copied field-guide prompt"
lane_1_critic_result.follow_up: No lane-1 follow-up needed for this packet item.

lane_4_scout_refinement.selected_feedback: After the first-session menu-focus fix, prove the first Route v2 ready-to-file state still teaches the station filing path without keeping map/replay pressure active.
evidence: Lane 1 already fixed the fresh/current-biome menu default in packet 155, so lane 4 should avoid retouching game.ts or broad runtime-smoke expectations.
src/test/field-requests.test.ts already covers the ordered Shore Shelter transect and ready copy, while High Pass coverage proves ready-to-file map/replay suppression late in the arc.

The missing feedback-facing guard is the first-session Shore Shelter route-state split: active world-map cue is allowed, ready-to-file keeps the notebook task but clears active outing, route marker, and replay label.
main_contract: Add a focused behavior-neutral test in src/test/field-requests.test.ts for Shore Shelter active versus ready-to-file route-state behavior.
Assert active Shore Shelter can expose Today: Shore Shelter and a route marker when route-marker support is selected.
Assert ready-to-file Shore Shelter keeps the M -> Field station -> Enter summary and journal task, while activeOuting, routeMarkerLocationId, and routeReplayLabel are null.
Assert filing the note still advances to forest-hidden-hollow.
non_goals: No first-session menu-focus changes; lane 1 already completed that.
No edits to game.ts, station pages, world-map rendering, guided-season copy, save schema, route definitions, science content, geometry, runtime-smoke, tutorial UI, route HUD, or support framework.
No broad feedback batching beyond this high-confidence route-state guard.

lane_4_main_result.summary: Added behavior-neutral Shore Shelter route-state coverage proving active route-marker/world-map cue behavior, ready-to-file station filing copy, ready journal task, ready marker/replay/outing suppression, and the post-file move to forest-hidden-hollow.
lane_4_main_result.runtime_changes: None; this pass is test-only for Route v2 state behavior.

lane_4_critic_result.summary: No blocker found. The Shore Shelter guard covers active route-marker/world-map cue behavior, ready-to-file station filing copy, ready journal task, ready active-outing/marker/replay suppression, and post-file advancement without runtime drift.
lane_4_critic_result.follow_up: No lane-4 follow-up needed for packet 155.

lane_3_scout_refinement.selected_feedback: After the fresh/current-biome menu-focus fix, prove the beach opener itself still carries the first Shore Shelter objective without competing travel prompts.
evidence: Lane 1 fixed the fresh starter menu so nextBiomeId: beach no longer defaults to world-map.
docs/alpha-screenshot-proof-manifest.md already treats first-session-beach-objective as a proof-first spatial checkpoint.
The latest first-session beach browser state shows active beach-shore-shelter, guided nextBiomeId: beach, dune-edge, nearby sand-verbena, nearbyDoor.inRange false, and nearbyTravelTarget null.
Focused beach and runtime-smoke slices already protect the opening shoulder carriers and local no-travel-target read.
main_contract: Capture fresh ignored browser proof under output/lane-3-main-428-spatial-feedback/ for first-session-beach-objective.png, first-session-beach-objective.json, and console-errors.json.

Assert or record that the frame is playing beach/dune-edge, activeFieldRequest.id is beach-shore-shelter, guidedFieldSeason.nextBiomeId is beach, a local opener carrier is nearby/visible, nearbyTravelTarget is null, and nearbyDoor.inRange is false.
If the proof is clean, make no runtime or geometry change and add an implementation report.
Only if the proof fails, make one tiny lane-3-owned reinforcement near the existing dune-shoulder-grass / dune-shoulder-entry-lip family and extend focused beach/opening-shoulder coverage.
non_goals: No first-session menu-focus change, route-state logic, field station, world-map focus, save schema, support behavior, route definitions, authored science facts, journal copy, or broader UI.
No new geometry unless the fresh proof shows the local beach objective cue no longer carries the opener.
recommended_checks: npm test -- --run src/test/beach-biome.test.ts -t "opening dune shoulder|authored beach clues|upper beach"
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "first field-season guidance|opening dune shoulder"

lane_3_main_result.summary: Captured fresh first-session beach objective browser proof after the menu-focus fix; no lane-3 geometry or runtime change was needed.
verification: one-off Playwright first-session beach objective proof against production preview
npm test -- --run src/test/beach-biome.test.ts -t "opening dune shoulder|authored beach clues|upper beach"
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "first field-season guidance|opening dune shoulder"
lane_3_main_result.runtime_changes: None; the proof passed and no geometry or source change was needed.

lane_3_critic_result.summary: No blocker found. The first-session beach objective proof confirms Shore Shelter remains readable from the local dune-edge space without a competing travel prompt or geometry fix.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/beach-biome.test.ts -t "opening dune shoulder|authored beach clues|upper beach"
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "first field-season guidance|opening dune shoulder"

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: Feedback items are triaged into keep/fix/defer, with only high-confidence fixes entering the lanes.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Triage systems/usability feedback and implement only small state/focus/save fixes with clear reproduction.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Triage comprehension/science/copy feedback and implement only relation-clarifying edits under budget.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Triage spatial confusion feedback and implement only small readability fixes to existing places.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Triage route/support/replay feedback and implement only fixes that preserve current Route v2 shell.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.
